parsed_review.summary: CP00-257 is a clean descriptor-only increment that closes the RP07.P06.M02 Type And Shape Definition tail (cross-tenant test, leak prevention test) and opens the M03 Primary Implementation Slice head (permission matrix row, view/search/mutation/export/share/AI-retrieval decision bindings, audit hint fields) over exactly 10 planned RP07 units. Pack identity, risk class A, unit count 10, range, deliverable distribution (test:2/security_audit:2/implementation:6), and micro-title distribution are identical across the prompt baseline, review-baseline.json, manifest plan_binding_snapshot + included_units, the live closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-257 entry, the registry SEARCH_CORE_CP257_PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS, and the contract current_pack + permission_slice_head_descriptor projection. All 7 required public exports are defined (service.js, validators.js) and re-exported via index.js wildcard. The implementation is genuinely descriptor-only: freezeCp257Result forces every runtime/write/dispatch flag false and no_real_data true; M03 row extras (no permission-decision detail, no AI dispatch, no audit-hint detail) are inherited from CP256_ROW_EXTRAS; and a code-level scan found zero write/network/DB/child_process operations in the search src or test. Coverage and descriptor validators, the test suite, and the rp07:search-core:validate script all bind to the live plan and contract and deepEqual the contract projection against the freshly generated descriptor, so drift would fail validation. The authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation false, deny_over_allow_enforced true, and Hermes appears only as a deterministic control-plane evidence-packet descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false; no product-runtime embedding). CP178+ hardening blockers (failed auth, zero-byte output, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims) are encoded in forbidden_review_evidence, and the readiness auth preflight passed with a non-empty valid-JSON result. The pack-level production_ready claim is the conservative value false (status implemented_pending_review), which is fully supported and does not weaken any gate. The downstream artifacts claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are not yet present, which is expected because they are produced after this read-only review step in the hardening sequence. One non-blocking P3 cosmetic observation on the generated changed-file-scope.json classification. No P0, P1, or P2 findings.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies pack's own untracked files as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-257/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 96-97) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 102-103). The field name 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved' is effectively 'all dirty files preserved (not reverted)' rather than strictly 'unrelated', so listing the pack's own untracked artifacts there is a naming/classification redundancy, not a functional defect. The load-bearing guarantees still hold: active_pack_implementation_refs correctly lists the 8 search files, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (nothing required was omitted), and the genuinely unrelated files (the .docx and the ldip-integration invalid-attempt JSON) are correctly preserved and not inferred into pack scope.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the scope generator so unrelated_dirty_files_preserved excludes files already classified as untracked_implementation_files (or rename the field to dirty_files_preserved) to remove the apparent contradiction. No change required for this pack's closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic/metadata observation in a generated review artifact; does not affect pack correctness, boundaries, or the production_ready claim. Safe to defer or ignore.
